•Assumes all critical components are approved for their intended markets and used within their 
intended ratings. Components to be provided with their respective datasheets, approval 
information/DoC/CoC/CoA/CoI. Additional limited evaluations of components (including power 
supply assumed to carry existing certifications) are not covered by the scope of this quote.
•Assumes device can be operated continuously without operator intervention.
•1 fully functional sample of each model variant, with any associated equipment/configurations 
for maximum heat rise.
•Includes all country deviations for CB certification
•Assumes testing on one model of product that represents worst case. If, after construction 
review this is not possible, a change order will be issued to cover testing of additional models.
•Assumes critical components are NRTL approved for their intended use and market.
•Assumes product is intended for indoor use.

Perform Air-Blank Test
1. In SCRAMNET, select the Inventory button and search 
for the device's serial number using the filters. 
2. Select the serial number hyperlink. 
3. Select the Initiate Air-Blank button. 
4. Wake up the device. No further action is required on 
your part. The device will step through the air-blank test 
process itself.  
5. Verify the Air-Blank test results appear on the Device 
Details page in SCRAMNET for the device.

Items.field:
General product information and other remarks:
Description of unit:
Items.value: The RB200 is a handheld, portable breath alcohol monitoring device designed for use in community corrections and related applications. It enables scheduled, random, on-demand, and client-initiated breath alcohol testing. The device is intended to improve outcomes in drunk and impaired driving cases, as well as domestic and family intervention scenarios. During operation, the RB200 powers up, prompts the user to test, captures a picture for identity verification, and uploads both the image and test results via a 4G LTE cellular module to a remote database. The device also supports Wi-Fi connectivity, which is used primarily when LTE is unavailable, such as in correctional facilities. The Wi-Fi and LTE modules operate exclusively to conserve battery life. The RB200 is designed to transmit data only after a breath test is completed, ensuring the device is not held to the face during transmission. Internal photos confirm a compact, integrated design with a camera, display, and processor PCB. The device is manufactured by Alcohol Monitoring Systems, Inc.

Items.value: There are two equipment models or variants identified: RB-200 and RB-210. The primary difference between these variants is the antenna band optimization for different cellular carriers. The RB-200 is optimized for AT&T, while the RB-210 is optimized for Verizon. Both models use the same sensor and processor PCBA, but the antenna trace is cut shorter for the Verizon (RB-210) version to match the required frequency bands. The marking label also confirms the existence of the RB-210 model, with the hardware version number (HVIN) listed as RB-200, indicating a shared hardware platform with carrier-specific modifications.

EXPOSITION HF: Cet appareil est un émetteur-récepteur radio. Il est conçu et fabriqué pour 
ne pas dépasser les limites d'émissions à l'exposition aux hautes fréquences (HF) établies 
par Industrie Canada (IC). Ces limites font partie de directives complètes et déterminent les 
niveaux autorisés d'énergie HF pour la population générale. Ces directives se fondent sur 
les normes de sécurité précédemment établies par Industrie Canada et les organismes de 
normalisation internationaux. Les normes comprennent une marge de sûreté substantielle 
conçue afin d'assurer la sécurité de toutes les personnes, quel que soient leur âge et leur état 
de santé.
L'exposition normalisée aux équipements HF sans fi l utilise une unité de mesure connue sous 
le nom de Débit d'absorption spécifi que ou DAS. La limite du DAS défi nie par IC est de 4 W/
kg. Les valeurs du DAS en dessous de cette limite sont considérées comme étant sans danger

If the device fails a diagnostic check, the device should be removed from 
service and returned to the manufacturer for maintenance.
• The device should be removed from service and returned to the 
manufacturer if the device fails to calibrate to the established 
specifications.
• The device should be removed from service and returned to the 
manufacturer if requested by an authorized AMS representative.

the device operator to ensure there are no signs of damage.
• Repairs to the device should only be conducted by trained AMS 
technicians.

• Product on the Restricted 
Product and Services List 
(Section 1) for which an 
exception is being submitted  
• Product containing hazardous 
materials (e.g. asbestos, 
known carcinogens, bio-
agents, industrial/high 
radiation sources) 
• Product makes a medical 
claim but is not being tested 
to medical standard 
• Child Appealing  
• Toys (may conflict with 
Marks provided by other 
Intertek Divisions)  
• Product tested to EN 
60335-2-9, EN 60335-2-
14 or EN 60335-2-24 for 
the European market 
must be referred to the 
local Certification Body 
prior to proceeding 
• Personal Protective 
Equipment 
• Solid Fuel Burning Appliances 
(solid fuel includes, but is not 
limited to: wood, coal, peat, 
grains, pellets)

and end products, may be inspected and tested by AMS at reasonable times before, during and after

returned to Seller at Seller's risk and expense and shall not be replaced by Seller without AMS's written 
authorization.

Perform Air-Blank Test
1. In SCRAMNET, select the Inventory button and search 
for the device's serial number using the filters. 
2. Select the serial number hyperlink. 
3. Select the Initiate Air-Blank button. 
4. Wake up the device. No further action is required on 
your part. The device will step through the air-blank test 
process itself.  
5. Verify the Air-Blank test results appear on the Device 
Details page in SCRAMNET for the device.

If the device fails a diagnostic check, the device should be removed from 
service and returned to the manufacturer for maintenance.
• The device should be removed from service and returned to the 
manufacturer if the device fails to calibrate to the established 
specifications.
• The device should be removed from service and returned to the 
manufacturer if requested by an authorized AMS representative.
• A physical inspection of the device should be conducted before testing by 
the device operator to ensure there are no signs of damage.
• Repairs to the device should only be conducted by trained AMS 
technicians.

ETL Listing Certification for North America:
Intertek will evaluate your product(s) to the applicable requirements of the US and Canadian standards 
referenced in this proposal. If your product(s) are found to be in compliance an ETL Listing Report will be 
issued. If non-compliances are found, we will issue format report to assist you with making corrective 
action.
Before the ETL Listing Mark can be applied to your products, we must execute a Certificate Agreement 
and establish Follow-up Services in you manufacturing locations. There are additional fees associated 
with maintaining the ETL Listing Mark. To learn more about this important step, please contact your 
Account Manager or visit http://intertek.com/guide-to-etl.
Pricing assumes all critical components are properly approved. If you have any questions regarding this 
assumption, please contact your account manager.

for maximum heat rise.

review this is not possible, a change order will be issued to cover testing of additional models.
•Assumes critical components are NRTL approved for their intended use and market.
•Assumes product is intended for indoor use.

EXPOSITION HF: Cet appareil est un émetteur-récepteur radio. Il est conçu et fabriqué pour 
ne pas dépasser les limites d'émissions à l'exposition aux hautes fréquences (HF) établies 
par Industrie Canada (IC). Ces limites font partie de directives complètes et déterminent les 
niveaux autorisés d'énergie HF pour la population générale. Ces directives se fondent sur 
les normes de sécurité précédemment établies par Industrie Canada et les organismes de 
normalisation internationaux. Les normes comprennent une marge de sûreté substantielle 
conçue afin d'assurer la sécurité de toutes les personnes, quel que soient leur âge et leur état 
de santé.
L'exposition normalisée aux équipements HF sans fi l utilise une unité de mesure connue sous 
le nom de Débit d'absorption spécifi que ou DAS. La limite du DAS défi nie par IC est de 4 W/
kg. Les valeurs du DAS en dessous de cette limite sont considérées comme étant sans danger

FCC RF Exposure Information: This device is a radio transmitter and receiver. It is designed 
and manufactured not to exceed the emissions limits for exposure to radio frequency (RF) 
energy set by the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) of the U.S. Government. These 
limits are part of comprehensive guidelines and establish permitted levels of RF energy for the 
general population. These guidelines are based on the safety standards previously set by the 
U.S. and international standards bodies. The standards include a substantial safety margin 
designed to assure the safety of all persons, regardless of age and health. 
The exposure standard for wireless RF devices employs a unit of measurement known as the 
Specific Absorption Rate, or SAR. The SAR limit set by the FCC is 4 W/kg. SAR values at or 
below that limit are considered safe for the general public. The maximum SAR value when 
operated in as a hand held device in accordance with this manual is 1.31 W/kg, which is below

Perform Air-Blank Test
1. In SCRAMNET, select the Inventory button and search 
for the device's serial number using the filters. 
2. Select the serial number hyperlink. 
3. Select the Initiate Air-Blank button. 
4. Wake up the device. No further action is required on 
your part. The device will step through the air-blank test 
process itself.  
5. Verify the Air-Blank test results appear on the Device 
Details page in SCRAMNET for the device.

If the device fails a diagnostic check, the device should be removed from 
service and returned to the manufacturer for maintenance.
• The device should be removed from service and returned to the 
manufacturer if the device fails to calibrate to the established 
specifications.
• The device should be removed from service and returned to the 
manufacturer if requested by an authorized AMS representative.
• A physical inspection of the device should be conducted before testing by 
the device operator to ensure there are no signs of damage.
• Repairs to the device should only be conducted by trained AMS 
technicians.

Notes and Assumptions:
•Assumes all critical components are approved for their intended markets and used within their 
intended ratings. Components to be provided with their respective datasheets, approval 
information/DoC/CoC/CoA/CoI. Additional limited evaluations of components (including power 
supply assumed to carry existing certifications) are not covered by the scope of this quote.
•Assumes device can be operated continuously without operator intervention.
•1 fully functional sample of each model variant, with any associated equipment/configurations 
for maximum heat rise.
•Includes all country deviations for CB certification
•Assumes testing on one model of product that represents worst case. If, after construction 
review this is not possible, a change order will be issued to cover testing of additional models.
•Assumes critical components are NRTL approved for their intended use and market.
•Assumes product is intended for indoor use.

text_support.preview_text:
Notes and Assumptions:
•Assumes all critical components are approved for their intended markets and used within their 
intended ratings. Components to be provided with their respective datasheets, approval 
information/DoC/CoC/CoA/CoI. Additional limited evaluations of components (including power 
supply assumed to carry existing certifications) are not covered by the scope of this quote.
•Assumes device can be operated continuously without operator intervention.
•1 fully functional sample of each model variant, with any associated equipment/configurations 
for maximum heat rise.
•Includes all country deviations for CB certification
•Assumes testing on one model of product that represents worst case. If, after construction 
review this is not possible, a change order will be issued to cover testing of additional models.
•Assumes critical components are NRTL approved for their intended use and market.
•Assumes product is intended for indoor use.

• Lawn Mowers 
• Radio Controlled 
Hoists/Cranes 
• Pressure Cookers 
• Deep Fat Fryers  
• Electric Blankets 
• Electric Slicers and Juicers 
• Ladders 
• Gas and Propane Products 
• Smoke, CO, Heat and Gas 
Alarms/Detectors  
• Fire Suppression Systems 
• Electric Fence Controllers (UL 69 
or CSA C22.2 No. 103) 
3.2 Service Type:  Will the service result in Intertek’s name, logo, certification mark, or other 
identifying information being placed on the product(s) or packaging? This would include:  
• Testing for any Intertek Certification Program (including construction review, preliminary design review, 
risk analysis, and limited production certification) or CB Scheme. If service is a Report Revision, select 
“No”. 
• Inspection Services (excludes Follow-up Services (FUS) inspections) 
☒YES    ☐NO

and/or app remote control for use with one of the following product types: a product or appliance 
intended to generate heat (e.g., cooking or personal care products; but does not include room or   
portable heaters), exercise equipment, spa/whirlpool, any product identified in RAF Section 3.1b 
List for ETL/WH Marks, or a new/unusual product?  May require special review, handling and additional 
oversight by Chief Engineering. 
☐YES    ☒NO 
If you answered “YES” to any question in Section 2, a quote may not be issued without written approval from Chief 
Engineering or Regional Compliance Officer (RCO).  Forward the RAF and Project Details to Chief Engineer and RCO.   
Chief Engineer Name:        
Chief Engineer Recommendation:         
 
 
SECTION 3: PROJECT RISK ASSESSMENT 
3.1 Product Type:  Is the product type one of the following? Review both 3.1.a and 3.1.b. ☐YES    ☒NO 
3.1.a All Intertek Marks 3.1.b ETL/WH Marks include the following in addition to 3.1.a

• Testing or certification of 
Drones (only FCC/IC TCB 
permitted with written 
authorization from RCO)  
• Electrical building inspection 
services for municipalities 
• Field evaluation of (1) direct 
gas-fired heaters, or  (2) 
explosion-proof electrical 
equipment and of intrinsically 
safe apparatus for use in 
hazardous (classified) 
locations, without written 
authorization from the Chief 
Engineer for that category  
• Certification under ETL 
Refurbishment Program of 
products for use in hazardous 
(classified) locations without 
written authorization from 
the Chief Engineer for that 
category 
 
1.2 Do the services include work for an attorney or use in litigation, an insurance claim or 
other commercial dispute?  
☐YES    ☒NO 
1.3 Is the client located in a Sanctioned Country or is a Specially Designated National on 
this list, or are the product(s) on which services will be performed originating from or 
destined for a Sanctioned Country?  LINK2

•Assumes all critical components are approved for their intended markets and used within their 
intended ratings. Components to be provided with their respective datasheets, approval 
information/DoC/CoC/CoA/CoI. Additional limited evaluations of components (including power 
supply assumed to carry existing certifications) are not covered by the scope of this quote.
•Assumes device can be operated continuously without operator intervention.
•1 fully functional sample of each model variant, with any associated equipment/configurations 
for maximum heat rise.
•Includes all country deviations for CB certification
•Assumes testing on one model of product that represents worst case. If, after construction 
review this is not possible, a change order will be issued to cover testing of additional models.
•Assumes critical components are NRTL approved for their intended use and market.
•Assumes product is intended for indoor use.

and/or app remote control for use with one of the following product types: a product or appliance 
intended to generate heat (e.g., cooking or personal care products; but does not include room or   
portable heaters), exercise equipment, spa/whirlpool, any product identified in RAF Section 3.1b 
List for ETL/WH Marks, or a new/unusual product?  May require special review, handling and additional 
oversight by Chief Engineering.

service and returned to the manufacturer for maintenance.

technicians.

(C) Bills of lading shall be mailed in triplicate to the destination address shown on the face of the P. O.

(D) All shipments are to be made F.O. B. destination, unless otherwise indicated on the face of the P.O.

not insure shipments for loss or damage, unless specifically directed to by AMS in writing. Charges 
resulting to AMS from Seller's failure to comply with the provisions of this paragraph shall be the sole 
liability of Seller.

Certification Training course.
Calibrate Device
Scan the QR code or visit https://help.scramnetwork.com/
documentation/remote-breath/equipment-management/ for 
detailed instructions. (SCRAMNET login required)
1. Ensure the calibration equipment is in good working order 
and the canister has an adequate gas level to complete 
the calibration and a calibration check.

If the device fails a diagnostic check, the device should be removed from 
service and returned to the manufacturer for maintenance.
• The device should be removed from service and returned to the 
manufacturer if the device fails to calibrate to the established 
specifications.
• The device should be removed from service and returned to the 
manufacturer if requested by an authorized AMS representative.
• A physical inspection of the device should be conducted before testing by 
the device operator to ensure there are no signs of damage.
• Repairs to the device should only be conducted by trained AMS 
technicians.

Notes and Assumptions:
•Assumes all critical components are approved for their intended markets and used within their 
intended ratings. Components to be provided with their respective datasheets, approval 
information/DoC/CoC/CoA/CoI. Additional limited evaluations of components (including power 
supply assumed to carry existing certifications) are not covered by the scope of this quote.
•Assumes device can be operated continuously without operator intervention.
•1 fully functional sample of each model variant, with any associated equipment/configurations 
for maximum heat rise.

•Assumes testing on one model of product that represents worst case. If, after construction 
review this is not possible, a change order will be issued to cover testing of additional models.
•Assumes critical components are NRTL approved for their intended use and market.
•Assumes product is intended for indoor use.

EXPOSITION HF: Cet appareil est un émetteur-récepteur radio. Il est conçu et fabriqué pour 
ne pas dépasser les limites d'émissions à l'exposition aux hautes fréquences (HF) établies 
par Industrie Canada (IC). Ces limites font partie de directives complètes et déterminent les 
niveaux autorisés d'énergie HF pour la population générale. Ces directives se fondent sur 
les normes de sécurité précédemment établies par Industrie Canada et les organismes de 
normalisation internationaux. Les normes comprennent une marge de sûreté substantielle 
conçue afin d'assurer la sécurité de toutes les personnes, quel que soient leur âge et leur état 
de santé.
L'exposition normalisée aux équipements HF sans fi l utilise une unité de mesure connue sous 
le nom de Débit d'absorption spécifi que ou DAS. La limite du DAS défi nie par IC est de 4 W/
kg. Les valeurs du DAS en dessous de cette limite sont considérées comme étant sans danger

Perform Air-Blank Test
1. In SCRAMNET, select the Inventory button and search 
for the device's serial number using the filters. 
2. Select the serial number hyperlink. 
3. Select the Initiate Air-Blank button.

Notes and Assumptions:
•Assumes all critical components are approved for their intended markets and used within their 
intended ratings. Components to be provided with their respective datasheets, approval 
information/DoC/CoC/CoA/CoI. Additional limited evaluations of components (including power 
supply assumed to carry existing certifications) are not covered by the scope of this quote.

for maximum heat rise.

review this is not possible, a change order will be issued to cover testing of additional models.

SCRAM Remote Breath Pro 
Quick Reference Guide
16
Device Calibration Requirements
1. Calibration must be performed by an AMS-certified calibration technician.
2. Devices are calibrated externally using a DOT standard 0.100 BAC 
dry gas that is included on the NHTSA Conforming Products List of 
Calibrating Units for Breath Alcohol Testers.
3. The device contains sensors that automatically compensate for 
environmental conditions such as temperature and altitude.
4. AMS recommends calibration at least once every 12 months or if the 
device fails a field calibration check. However, customers may want to 
calibrate more frequently based on their own internal policies, state laws, 
or local court/agency policies.
5. Calibration intervals can be set in SCRAMNET for notification when 
calibration is due.
6. All devices will be calibrated by the manufacturer utilizing DOT standard 
0.100 BAC dry gas before the original shipment of the device or after the 
device has been repaired.

0.100 BAC dry gas before the original shipment of the device or after the 
device has been repaired.
Device Calibration Check Requirements
1. Calibration checks must be performed by an AMS-certified calibration 
technician.
2. Perform calibration check using DOT standard 0.100 BAC dry gas.
3. Calibration check must be within ± 0.005 BAC of the 0.100 alcohol 
standard utilized.
4. Perform calibration check between user assignments (recommended).
Safety and Handling Instructions
Silencing Device: Access the volume controls through the menu. When the 
volume is turned all the way down, the device will vibrate when a breath test or 
charging is required.
Carrying and Handling Device: The device should always be transported in the 
carrying case.
Water and Wet Locations: The device should not be exposed to water.
24/7 Customer Service
Phone: (303) 785-7879 
E-mail: support@scramsystems.com

SCRAM Remote Breath Pro 
Quick Reference Guide
15
Product Specifications
General Information
Operating Temperature Range: 32-122F (0-50C)
Recommended Storage Temperature 
Range:
32-122F (0-50C)
Measurement Range: 0.000 – 0.400 BrAC
Breath Sample Volume: 1.5L
Accuracy: +/-0.005 up to 0.100 BrAC 
+/-5% from 0.100 to 0.400 BrAC
Alcohol Sensor: Proven Fuel Cell Technology
Automated Facial Intelligence (AFI): Proprietary 1-to-1 facial template 
matching
Battery: Rechargeable Lithium Ion
Input Power: 5VDC @ 2.0A, 10.0 W (0.4A max)
Best results are obtained with no more than +/- 15 degrees head roll, pitch, or 
yaw; neutral face expression; no sunglasses or heavy-framed glasses; and no 
hair in the face.
Maintenance, Inspection, and Device 
Return Conditions
• The device conducts a series of diagnostic checks each time it wakes up. 
If the device fails a diagnostic check, the device should be removed from 
service and returned to the manufacturer for maintenance.
